headline: Bus Shelter Ads
subtext: Ratepayers fund the council's creative waste.
body:
Warringah's Council Chambers have recently come under fire for their latest decision to place ads inside bus shelters. According to the council, this change will help local businesses thrive by providing them with more advertising opportunities.

The public has been less than pleased with the new development. "It's like they've taken a page out of New York City's playbook and decided to make us all pay for their art projects," says a disgruntled local ratepayer from The Corso.

Many locals, particularly those in Manly, are taking matters into their own hands by covering or defacing the ads. "I can't believe we're resorting to graffiti again! It's 2005, folks!" exclaims an irate resident at The Steyne Pub.

Meanwhile, Warringah Council has decided to press on with these bus shelter advertisements. "If people don't want to see ads when they wait for their bus, all they have to do is avert their eyes," remarks the council's spokesperson in a statement that has been met with more eye-rolling than support.

The B-Line Bus Depot workers are even planning a strike to protest these garish intrusions. "It's bad enough we have to wear uniforms and stand around looking professional for our day jobs, but now we're expected to be walking billboards too?" grumbles one of the bus drivers.

Time will tell if this new development becomes as ubiquitous as Warringah Council Chambers' questionable choice of paint colours or fades into obscurity like last season's COVID protocols.

professor.title: From The Archives
professor.content: The archives are currently closed due to a flea infestation.

classifieds: For Sale: Ephemeral Beachfront Property. $1 million
Found: Sense of humor. Last seen in a comedy club.
Services: We'll provide sarcastic comments during your vacation.

mystic.prediction: Aries of Northern Beaches Sydney, beware of parking issues on your way to caffeinating at the local beachside cafe; seek wisdom from the surf gods for balance amidst real estate market fluctuations, and remember to take the B-line bus instead of driving to avoid the wrath of the Bin Chickens who have their own schedule to follow.
